remember: Students will be able to recall the basic concepts of Linear Programming, its importance, and the types of problems that can be solved using Linear Programming.
Students will be able to recall the different methods of solving Linear Programming problems, such as the graphical method and the simplex method.
Students will be able to recall the basic terminology and notation used in Linear Programming, such as variables, constraints, objective functions, and feasible region.
understand: Students will be able to understand the concept of feasible region and its importance in Linear Programming.
Students will be able to understand how to determine the optimal solution to a Linear Programming problem using the graphical method.
Students will be able to understand the basic steps involved in solving a Linear Programming problem using the simplex method.
apply: Students will be able to apply the graphical method to solve simple Linear Programming problems.
Students will be able to apply the simplex method to solve simple Linear Programming problems.
Students will be able to use software or calculators to solve Linear Programming problems and interpret the results.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze a Linear Programming problem and determine the optimal solution using various methods.
Students will be able to analyze the sensitivity of the optimal solution to changes in the coefficients of the objective function and constraints.
Students will be able to analyze the implications of the optimal solution on the resources and constraints of the problem.

ncert_objectives: To understand the basic concepts of Linear Programming and its importance.
To solve Linear Programming problems using the graphical method and the simplex method.
To determine the optimal solution to a Linear Programming problem.
board_exam_objectives: To solve Linear Programming problems using the graphical method and the simplex method.
To determine the optimal solution to a Linear Programming problem and interpret the results.
To analyze the sensitivity of the optimal solution to changes in the coefficients of the objective function and constraints.
